tools.name: githubSearchCode
tools.description:
## Search GitHub code [EXTERNAL: GitHub API]
  <when>
  - Find code patterns | Locate files by path
  - match="file" -> content | match="path" -> file names
  - External research | Cross-repo patterns
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - githubSearchRepositories: After finding repo
  - githubViewRepoStructure: After understanding layout
  - packageSearch: After finding package
  - self: Refine query | Switch match=file<->path
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - githubGetFileContent: Read matched files (text_matches for matchString)
  - githubViewRepoStructure: Explore around matches
  - githubSearchPullRequests: Find history
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - 1-2 filters safe. NEVER combine extension+filename+path
  - path is strict prefix: "pkg" finds pkg/file, NOT parent/pkg/file
  - Start lean: single filter -> verify -> add filters
  - Prefer owner+repo for precision
  - For packages: packageSearch first
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - owner="facebook", repo="react", match="path", keywordsToSearch=["utils"]
  - keywordsToSearch=["useState"], match="file"
  - path="src/api", extension="ts", keywordsToSearch=["export"]
  </examples>

queries.description: Research queries for githubSearchCode (1-3 queries per call for optimal resource management). Review schema before use for optimal results

id.description: Stable query identifier used to match each result to its input query. Required for every query. Use a short meaningful string like "react_hooks_search" or "router_prs".

mainResearchGoal.description: Main research objective (multiple queries can share the same main goal for semantic grouping and session tracking)

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

keywordsToSearch.description: Search terms (1-5, AND logic). match=file returns text_matches[]

charOffset.description: Character offset for output pagination after domain pagination has been applied.

charLength.description: Character budget for output pagination after domain pagination has been applied.

responseCharLength.description: Character budget for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Overrides the shared default for this call.

status.description: Indicates the query returned results successfully

tools.name: githubGetFileContent
tools.description:
## Read GitHub file or download directory [EXTERNAL: GitHub API]
  <when>
  - Read file from known path (after discovery/search)
  - External library implementation research
  - Read configs, docs, or source from GitHub repos
  - Download entire directory for local analysis (type="directory")
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - githubSearchCode: Read from results (use text_matches for matchString)
  - githubViewRepoStructure: Read discovered paths
  - packageSearch: Read package source
  - self: Expand context
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - githubSearchCode: Search imports/patterns
  - githubSearchPullRequests: Find file history
  - packageSearch: Look up imports
  - localSearchCode/localViewStructure: After type="directory" download
  </toTool>
  <modes>
  - type="file" (default): Returns content inline. Use matchString/startLine/fullContent
  - type="directory": Downloads all files to disk, returns localPath for local tools
  </modes>
  <gotchas>
  - Choose ONE: matchString OR startLine+endLine OR fullContent (file mode only)
  - Large files: Use matchString for token efficiency
  - 300KB max (FILE_TOO_LARGE error)
  - Branch: Use NAME (e.g., "main"), not SHA
  - type="directory" ignores content params (startLine, matchString, etc.)
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - matchString="export function", matchStringContextLines=20
  - startLine=1, endLine=50
  - fullContent=true (small configs only)
  - type="directory", path="src/utils" (downloads dir for local analysis)
  </examples>

queries.description: Research queries for githubGetFileContent (1-3 queries per call for optimal resource management). Review schema before use for optimal results

id.description: Stable query identifier used to match each result to its input query. Required for every query. Use a short meaningful string like "react_hooks_search" or "router_prs".

mainResearchGoal.description: Main research objective (multiple queries can share the same main goal for semantic grouping and session tracking)

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

path.description: File or directory path from root, no leading slash, exact case

type.description: Choose ONE: "file" (default) returns content inline; "directory" saves all files to disk and returns localPath. Directory mode requires ENABLE_LOCAL=true and ENABLE_CLONE=true, and is only available with the GitHub provider (not GitLab).

fullContent.description: Return entire file (default: false). File mode only

startLine.description: Start line (with endLine). File mode only

endLine.description: End line (with startLine). File mode only

matchString.description: Search pattern to extract. File mode only

charOffset.description: Pagination offset. Default: 0

charLength.description: Max chars per page. Default: 20000

forceRefresh.description: When true, bypass the cache and force a fresh fetch even if a valid cached copy exists. Only relevant for type "directory".

responseCharOffset.description: Character offset for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Use when a multi-query response was auto-paginated.

responseCharLength.description: Character budget for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Overrides the shared default for this call.

tools.name: githubViewRepoStructure
tools.description:
## Display GitHub repo structure [EXTERNAL: GitHub API]
  <when>
  - Understand layout | Discover paths | Entry point
  - Workflow: Root (depth=1) -> Drill (path+depth=2) -> Search/Read
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - githubSearchRepositories: After finding repo
  - packageSearch: After finding package
  - self: Drill into subdirectory
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - githubSearchCode: Search in discovered dirs
  - githubGetFileContent: Read discovered files
  - githubSearchPullRequests: Find changes
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - Start at root (path="", depth=1) first
  - depth=2 slow on large dirs - use on subdirs
  - Monorepos: Check packages/, apps/, libs/
  - Auto-filters noisy dirs (.git, node_modules, dist)
  - Max 200 items - check summary.truncated
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - path="", depth=1 (root)
  - path="src", depth=2 (drill into source)
  - path="packages/core", depth=1 (monorepo)
  </examples>

tools.name: githubSearchRepositories
tools.description:
## Search GitHub repositories [EXTERNAL: GitHub API]
  <when>
  - External research | Finding repos | Discovering projects
  - Keywords for org repos, topics for open-source
  - Known packages: use packageSearch first (faster)
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - packageSearch: After finding package, explore repo
  - githubSearchCode: Found code? Find origin repo
  - self: Refine query | Switch topics<->keywords
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - githubViewRepoStructure: Explore structure
  - githubSearchCode: Search code
  - githubGetFileContent: Read files
  - githubSearchPullRequests: Find changes
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - Check pushedAt (code change) > updatedAt (meta change)
  - stars >1000 filters noise (may hide new projects)
  - Try synonyms: auth<->authentication, plugin<->extension
  - Archived repos auto-excluded
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - topicsToSearch=["typescript", "cli"], stars=">1000"
  - owner="bgauryy", keywordsToSearch=["auth-service"]
  </examples>

tools.name: githubSearchPullRequests
tools.description:
## Search GitHub Pull Requests [EXTERNAL: GitHub API]
  <when>
  - Code archaeology: WHY was code written this way?
  - Implementation history | Review changes | Find introducing PR
  - Workflow: type=metadata first -> type=partialContent for files
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - githubSearchCode: Found code? Find PRs that changed it
  - githubGetFileContent: Found file? Find change history
  - self: Refine query | Widen date range
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - githubGetFileContent: Read current state
  - githubSearchCode: Search patterns from patches
  - self: Deep dive with prNumber + type=partialContent
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - prNumber ignores ALL other filters
  - type=metadata first (fast), then partialContent
  - Avoid fullContent on large PRs (token expensive)
  - withComments=true for understanding WHY
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - prNumber=123, type="metadata"
  - owner="org", repo="repo", state="closed", merged=true
  - prNumber=123, type="partialContent", partialContentMetadata=[{"file": "src/app.ts"}]
  </examples>

queries.description: Research queries for githubSearchPullRequests (1-3 queries per call for optimal resource management). Review schema before use for optimal results

id.description: Stable query identifier used to match each result to its input query. Required for every query. Use a short meaningful string like "react_hooks_search" or "router_prs".

mainResearchGoal.description: Main research objective (multiple queries can share the same main goal for semantic grouping and session tracking)

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

query.description: Search across title, body, comments (max 256 chars)

state.description: Filter by PR state. "open" = open PRs. "closed" = closed PRs (includes merged). "merged" = merged PRs only (shorthand for closed + merged:true).

file.description: File path within the PR diff to fetch partial content for

additions.description: Specific addition line numbers to include from the diff

deletions.description: Specific deletion line numbers to include from the diff

responseCharLength.description: Character budget for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Overrides the shared default for this call.

additions.description: Lines added across the pull request

tools.name: localSearchCode
tools.description:
## Search code patterns [LOCAL: ripgrep] - START HERE for code questions
  <when>
  - Code questions, symbol lookups, pattern finding
  - Find functions, classes, variables, imports, constants
  - Get lineHint for LSP tools (if available)
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - localViewStructure: After understanding layout
  - localFindFiles: After narrowing by metadata
  - self: Refine pattern | Try variants
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  If LSP available:
  1. This tool gives file + lineHint
  2. lspGotoDefinition(lineHint) -> locate definition
  3. lspFindReferences -> all usages
  4. lspCallHierarchy -> trace call flow
  5. localGetFileContent -> read impl (last)
  
  Without LSP:
  - localGetFileContent(matchString) for context
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - lineHint is 1-indexed (first line = 1)
  - filesOnly=true for fast discovery
  - type filter (ts, js, py) faster than globs
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - path="/workspace/src", pattern="function handleAuth", type="ts"
  - path="/workspace", pattern="export.*UserService", perlRegex=true
  - path="/workspace", pattern="TODO", filesOnly=true
  </examples>

queries.description: Queries for localSearchCode (1–5 per call). Review schema before use.

id.description: Stable query identifier used to match each result to its input query. Required for every query. Use a short meaningful string like "react_hooks_search" or "router_prs".

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

matchContentLength.description: Maximum characters per individual match snippet. Default 200, max 50000. Raise this when matches sit on very long lines (minified code, JSON blobs, generated SQL). Total output size is still bounded by charLength / responseCharLength budgets — prefer paginating via filePageNumber/matchesPerPage over truncating a single match.

charOffset.description: Character offset for output pagination after file/match pagination has been applied.

charLength.description: Character budget for output pagination after file/match pagination has been applied.

verbosity.description: Choose response size. compact (default): files[] with path:line matches, snippets, match counts, search engine, and pagination; use for normal work and follow-up line hints. ultra: match/file counts plus the top path:line; files[] and match snippets are dropped; use first for broad/large probes when counts or top locations are enough. verbose: currently same as compact; skip it unless future docs say it adds detail. Drill-back from ultra: re-call with verbosity:"compact" or scope path/include to the top path.

responseCharOffset.description: Character offset for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Use when a multi-query response was auto-paginated.

responseCharLength.description: Character budget for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Overrides the shared default for this call.

tools.name: localViewStructure
tools.description:
## View directory structure [LOCAL: filesystem]
  <when>
  - Understand layout | Discover paths | Entry point
  - Unfamiliar codebase: Start depth=1
  - Workflow: Root (depth=1) -> Drill (depth=2) -> Search
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - self: Drill into subdirs
  - localFindFiles: Filter by metadata
  - localSearchCode: Search content
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  Source dirs found:
  1. localSearchCode(pattern) -> lineHint
  2. lspGotoDefinition (if LSP available)
  3. lspCallHierarchy/lspFindReferences
  4. localGetFileContent (last)
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - depth=2 slow on large dirs - use on subdirs
  - Auto-excludes: node_modules, .git, dist
  - Monorepos: Check packages/, apps/, libs/
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - path="/workspace", depth=1 (root)
  - path="/workspace/src", depth=2 (drill)
  - pattern="*.ts", filesOnly=true
  </examples>

queries.description: Queries for localViewStructure (1–5 per call). Review schema before use.

tools.name: localFindFiles
tools.description:
## Find files by metadata [LOCAL: filesystem]
  <when>
  - Find by name, time, size, permissions
  - Scope narrowing before content search
  - Recent changes: modifiedWithin='7d'
  - NOT for content - use localSearchCode
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - localViewStructure: After structure discovery
  - localSearchCode: Next step for content
  - self: Refine filters
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  Source files found:
  1. localSearchCode(pattern) -> lineHint
  2. lspGotoDefinition (if LSP available)
  3. lspCallHierarchy/lspFindReferences
  4. localGetFileContent (last)
  
  Config/docs: localGetFileContent directly
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - Faster than content search (metadata indexing)
  - excludeDir for noise (node_modules, dist, .git)
  - Time: '7d', '2h', '30m'
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - path="/workspace/src", name="*.ts", modifiedWithin="7d"
  - path="/workspace", iname="readme", type="f"
  - path="/workspace", sizeGreater="10M", excludeDir=["node_modules"]
  </examples>

tools.name: localGetFileContent
tools.description:
## Read file content [LOCAL: filesystem]
  <when>
  - Read implementation after locating with search/LSP
  - Read config/docs/non-code files directly
  - Get context around specific patterns with matchString
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - localSearchCode: Found file location
  - lspGotoDefinition: Located definition (if LSP available)
  - lspFindReferences/lspCallHierarchy: After analysis
  - Direct read OK for: configs, docs, .json, .md, .yaml
  </fromTool>
  <flow_questions>
  If LSP available, prefer LSP tools for:
  - "Who calls this?" -> lspCallHierarchy(incoming)
  - "Where is X used?" -> lspFindReferences
  - "Trace flow" -> lspCallHierarchy chain
  </flow_questions>
  <gotchas>
  - Source code: Use localSearchCode first, LSP if available
  - Large files: Use matchString for token efficiency
  - Known ranges: Use startLine/endLine
  - Small configs: fullContent=true is OK
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - path="/workspace/src/services/user.ts", matchString="class UserService", matchStringContextLines=10
  - path="/workspace/src/utils.ts", startLine=50, endLine=100 (known range)
  - path="/workspace/config.json", fullContent=true (small configs only)
  </examples>

tools.name: lspGotoDefinition
tools.description:
## Navigate to symbol definition [LOCAL: LSP]
  REQUIRES lineHint - call localSearchCode FIRST to get line number
  
  <when>
  - Find where function/class/variable/type is DEFINED
  - Jump from usage to declaration | Trace import to source
  - Flow: SEARCH -> LOCATE (this) -> ANALYZE -> READ
  </when>
  <prerequisite>
  - Need lineHint from localSearchCode (1-indexed)
  - symbolName must be EXACT (case-sensitive)
  </prerequisite>
  <fromTool>
  - localSearchCode: Provides lineHint (required)
  - self: Chain definitions (follow imports)
  - lspFindReferences/lspCallHierarchy: Jump to locations
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - lspFindReferences: Find ALL usages
  - lspCallHierarchy(incoming): Find callers
  - lspCallHierarchy(outgoing): Find callees
  - localGetFileContent: Read impl (last step)
  </toTool>
  <gotchas>
  - lineHint required (1-indexed from localSearchCode)
  - symbolName must be EXACT (no parentheses)
  - Tool searches +/-2 lines if not exact match
  - External package? packageSearch -> githubGetFileContent
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - uri="src/services/auth.ts", symbolName="handleLogin", lineHint=42
  - uri="src/utils/index.ts", symbolName="formatDate", lineHint=15
  </examples>

queries.description: Queries for lspGotoDefinition (1–5 per call). Review schema before use.

id.description: Stable query identifier used to match each result to its input query. Required for every query. Use a short meaningful string like "react_hooks_search" or "router_prs".

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

symbolName.description: EXACT symbol text. No parens, no partial matches

orderHint.description: 0-indexed occurrence if multiple on line. Default: 0 Counts only code occurrences on the exact line (0-indexed); string/comment text is ignored.

charOffset.description: Character offset for query-level output pagination. Use when this LSP result was auto-paginated and you need the next window.

charLength.description: Character budget for query-level output pagination window.

verbosity.description: Choose response size. compact (default): definition locations with ranges, snippets, resolved position, and semantic/fallback mode; use for normal work and follow-up line hints. ultra: definition count plus top path:line:column; location content is empty; use first for broad/large probes when counts or top locations are enough. verbose: currently same as compact; skip it unless future docs say it adds detail. Drill-back from ultra: re-call with verbosity:"compact" for snippets around the location.

responseCharOffset.description: Character offset for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Use when a multi-query response was auto-paginated.

responseCharLength.description: Character budget for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Overrides the shared default for this call.

tools.name: lspFindReferences
tools.description:
## Find all usages of a symbol [LOCAL: LSP]
  REQUIRES lineHint - call localSearchCode FIRST to get line number
  
  <when>
  - "Where is X used?" - ALL usages of function/class/variable/type
  - Impact analysis - what breaks if I change this?
  - Semantic search (better than text matching)
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - localSearchCode: Get lineHint first (required)
  - lspGotoDefinition: After locating, find usages
  - self: Paginate (page=2,3...)
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - lspCallHierarchy: For call relationships (functions)
  - lspGotoDefinition: Jump to reference location
  - localGetFileContent: Read context (last step)
  </toTool>
  <vs_lspCallHierarchy>
  - lspFindReferences: ALL usages (calls, assignments, type refs)
  - lspCallHierarchy: CALL relationships only (functions)
  </vs_lspCallHierarchy>
  <gotchas>
  - lineHint required (1-indexed from localSearchCode)
  - symbolName must be EXACT (case-sensitive)
  - includeDeclaration=false for usages only
  - Finds dynamic usages text search misses
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - uri="src/types.ts", symbolName="UserConfig", lineHint=15
  - uri="src/utils.ts", symbolName="formatDate", lineHint=42, includeDeclaration=false
  </examples>

queries.description: Queries for lspFindReferences (1–5 per call). Review schema before use.

id.description: Stable query identifier used to match each result to its input query. Required for every query. Use a short meaningful string like "react_hooks_search" or "router_prs".

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

uri.description: File path containing the symbol. Example: "src/api/client.ts"

symbolName.description: EXACT symbol text. No parens, no partial matches

orderHint.description: 0-indexed occurrence if multiple on line. Default: 0

includeDeclaration.description: Include definition in results. Default: true

includePattern.description: Glob patterns to include (e.g. ["**/*.test.ts", "**/src/**"]). Only matching files returned

excludePattern.description: Glob patterns to exclude (e.g. ["**/node_modules/**", "**/dist/**"]). Matching files removed

verbosity.description: Choose response size. compact (default): reference locations with ranges, snippets, definition markers, pagination, and semantic/fallback mode; use for normal work and follow-up line hints. ultra: reference counts plus path:line refs, or a per-file rollup with groupByFile; snippets are dropped; use first for broad/large probes when counts or top locations are enough. verbose: currently same as compact; skip it unless future docs say it adds detail. Drill-back from ultra: re-call with verbosity:"compact", groupByFile, or includePattern.

groupByFile.description: Roll up references into per-file counts ({ "src/foo.ts": 7 }) instead of returning individual locations. Less tokens than the full locations list — use for impact-analysis ("is this used widely?"). Drill-back: re-query with `includePattern` scoped to the top file(s).

tools.name: lspCallHierarchy
tools.description:
## Trace function call relationships [LOCAL: LSP]
  REQUIRES lineHint - call localSearchCode FIRST to get line number
  
  <when>
  - "Who calls this?" -> direction="incoming"
  - "What does this call?" -> direction="outgoing"
  - Trace code/data flow | Impact analysis
  </when>
  <prerequisite>
  - Need lineHint from localSearchCode (1-indexed)
  - Symbol must be function/method (for types/variables use lspFindReferences)
  </prerequisite>
  <fromTool>
  - localSearchCode: Provides lineHint (required)
  - lspGotoDefinition: Locate definition first
  - self: Chain to trace deeper
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - self: Chain on callers/callees for full flow
  - lspGotoDefinition: Jump to call site
  - localGetFileContent: Read impl (last step)
  </toTool>
  <flow_pattern>
  Flow tracing: localSearchCode -> lspGotoDefinition -> lspCallHierarchy(outgoing) -> chain until target
  INCOMING: A <- [callers] | OUTGOING: A -> [callees]
  </flow_pattern>
  <vs_lspFindReferences>
  - lspCallHierarchy: CALL relationships (function calls)
  - lspFindReferences: ALL usages (calls, assignments, type refs)
  </vs_lspFindReferences>
  <gotchas>
  - lineHint required (1-indexed from localSearchCode)
  - depth=1 recommended (chain manually for deeper)
  - Works cross-file, traces imports automatically
  - Semantic: actual calls, not string matches
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - direction="incoming", uri="src/api.ts", symbolName="handleRequest", lineHint=50
  - direction="outgoing", uri="src/service.ts", symbolName="processData", lineHint=25
  </examples>

queries.description: Queries for lspCallHierarchy (1–5 per call). Review schema before use.

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

lineHint.description: 1-indexed line number where function is defined or called

orderHint.description: 0-indexed occurrence if multiple on line. Default: 0

charLength.description: Character budget for query-level output pagination window.

verbosity.description: Choose response size. compact (default): target item plus caller/callee nodes, snippets, call ranges, pagination, and semantic/fallback mode; use for normal work and follow-up line hints. ultra: edge counts and a compact A -> B edge list; node content and call arrays are dropped; use first for broad/large probes when counts or top locations are enough. verbose: currently same as compact; skip it unless future docs say it adds detail. Drill-back from ultra: re-call with verbosity:"compact" for full per-node context.

responseCharOffset.description: Character offset for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Use when a multi-query response was auto-paginated.

tools.name: packageSearch
tools.description:
## Find NPM/Python packages [EXTERNAL: npm/PyPI]
  <when>
  - Look up package by name (prevents research loops)
  - Get repository URL (owner/repo) for source exploration
  - Compare alternatives | Check deprecation
  </when>
  <fromTool>
  - localSearchCode: Found import? Look up package
  - localGetFileContent: Found package.json? Look up dependencies
  - self: Compare alternatives
  </fromTool>
  <toTool>
  - githubViewRepoStructure: Explore package source
  - githubSearchCode: Search implementation
  - githubGetFileContent: Read source files
  </toTool>
  <vsGitHub>
  - packageSearch: Fast lookup by name -> repo URL
  - githubSearchRepositories: Broad discovery by keywords
  Use packageSearch first for known package names
  </vsGitHub>
  <gotchas>
  - searchLimit=1 for known name | 5 for alternatives
  - Python: Always returns 1 result (PyPI limitation)
  - NPM uses dashes, Python uses underscores
  - Check DEPRECATED warnings first
  </gotchas>
  <examples>
  - ecosystem="npm", name="express"
  - ecosystem="python", name="requests"
  - ecosystem="npm", name="lodash", searchLimit=5
  </examples>

queries.description: Queries for packageSearch (1–5 per call). Review schema before use.

id.description: Stable query identifier used to match each result to its input query. Required for every query. Use a short meaningful string like "react_hooks_search" or "router_prs".

mainResearchGoal.description: Main research objective (multiple queries can share the same main goal for semantic grouping and session tracking)

reasoning.description: Why this query helps achieve the goal

searchLimit.description: Max results for NPM (1-10). Python: always 1. Default: 1

charOffset.description: Character offset for output pagination.

charLength.description: Character budget for output pagination.

responseCharOffset.description: Character offset for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Use when a multi-query response was auto-paginated.

responseCharLength.description: Character budget for top-level bulk response pagination across results[]. Overrides the shared default for this call.

results.description: Array of results, one per input query, discriminated by status

id.description: Stable query identifier matching the input query id

status.description: Indicates the query returned results successfully

data.description: The tool-specific result data

owner.description: Repository owner parsed from the repository URL

repo.description: Repository name parsed from the repository URL

path.description: NPM package path or repository subdirectory

mainEntry.description: Main entry point for NPM packages

typeDefinitions.description: Type definition entry point for NPM packages

weeklyDownloads.description: Weekly download count from npm registry

totalFound.description: Total number of packages matching the search query

outputPagination.description: Payload pagination metadata when the package list was size-paginated

status.description: Indicates the query returned no matching results
